title: Add Memory Test Game to Brain Training App

requirements.text: Add a new 'Memory Test' game type to the backend GameType variant and support saving its game state and high scores alongside existing game types.

quotes: koi 10 object screen par dikhe aur 10 secend baad gaayab ho jaye fi torant ek search baar aay aur 25 sec ka time mile

acceptanceCriteria: GameType variant includes a `memoryTest` entry
Backend can store and retrieve game state and high scores for memoryTest game type
Existing game types and data are unaffected

requirements.text: Create a useMemoryTest custom React hook that manages the Memory Test game logic: displays 10 everyday objects (with emoji + text) for 10 seconds, then hides them and starts a 25-second countdown, accepts user-typed object names one at a time via Enter key, tracks how many of the 10 objects have been correctly recalled, and determines win (all 10 recalled before time runs out) or loss (timer reaches zero).

quotes: koi 10 object screen par dikhe aur 10 secend baad gaayab ho jaye fi torant ek search baar aay aur 25 sec ka time mile agar ugers ne 10 secend mein un saari object ko note bar mein note kar diya hai to wah jeet jaata agar time out hota hai toh woh haar jaata hai

acceptanceCriteria: Hook exposes game phase: 'memorize' | 'recall' | 'won' | 'lost'
During 'memorize' phase, 10 objects are visible with a 10-second countdown
After 10 seconds, phase transitions to 'recall' and objects are hidden
During 'recall' phase, a 25-second countdown is active
User can type an object name, press Enter, and the hook validates it (case-insensitive)
Correctly recalled objects are tracked and displayed
Phase transitions to 'won' when all 10 objects are correctly recalled
Phase transitions to 'lost' when the 25-second timer expires
Hook provides a reset function to restart the game

requirements.text: Create a MemoryTestGame page at a new route (e.g., /memory-test) that renders the Memory Test game UI: shows a grid of 10 object cards (emoji + label) during the memorize phase with a 10-second countdown, then hides the objects and shows a search/input bar with a 25-second timer during the recall phase, displays a list of already-recalled objects, and shows a win/loss result screen on completion. Save the result to the backend using the useCompleteLevel mutation hook on game end.

quotes: koi 10 object screen par dikhe aur 10 secend baad gaayab ho jaye fi torant ek search baar aay aur 25 sec ka time mile

acceptanceCriteria: Route /memory-test renders the MemoryTestGame page
Memorize phase shows 10 object cards in a responsive grid with emoji and text label, and a visible 10-second countdown
After 10 seconds, cards disappear and a text input (search bar) appears
Recall phase shows the 25-second timer prominently
User can type an object name and press Enter to submit; correctly recalled objects appear in a list below the input
Invalid or duplicate entries are silently ignored or show a subtle error
Win screen appears when all 10 objects are recalled; loss screen appears when the timer expires
Game result is saved to the backend via useCompleteLevel
Restart button resets the game back to the memorize phase
Back-to-menu button navigates to the home page

requirements.text: Register the /memory-test route in App.tsx and add the Memory Test game card to the HomePage below all existing game cards, using a consistent GameCard component with the game's title 'Memory Test', a short description, and an appropriate icon.

quotes: ye game un saare game ke neeche hi add karna hai

acceptanceCriteria: App.tsx includes a route for /memory-test pointing to MemoryTestGame page
HomePage displays a Memory Test GameCard below all existing game cards
GameCard shows title 'Memory Test', a short description, and an icon
Clicking the card navigates to /memory-test

requirements.text: Add a difficulty configuration entry for 'memoryTest' in difficultyConfig.ts with appropriate settings across 5 levels (e.g., varying the number of objects shown, or time limits in future levels), defaulting to 10 objects, 10-second memorize phase, and 25-second recall phase at level 1.
acceptanceCriteria: difficultyConfig.ts exports configuration for memoryTest game type
Level 1 config specifies 10 objects, 10-second memorize timer, 25-second recall timer
5 levels are defined with progressively increasing difficulty

constraints: All existing games (Card Matching, Sliding Puzzle, Pattern Memory, Sequence Memory, Stroop Effect) must remain fully functional and unmodified.
Memory Test game card must appear below all existing game cards on the HomePage.
Do not edit any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Changing the visual theme or design of existing games
Adding partial credit scoring (win requires all 10 correct)

required: Memory test game icon showing a brain with magnifying glass or eye symbol, flat illustration style matching the app's purple/blue theme (memory-test-icon.dim_128x128.png)
